comments.text: I would like to mention the recent preprint of Eric Li, “The Sylow Divisor Condition: a Resolution of Erdős Problem 768”. It appears to settle Problem #768. In the notation of the page, Li proves $|A \cap [1,x]|/x = \exp(-(c+o(1))\sqrt{\log x}\log\log x)$ with the explicit constant $c = 1/(2\sqrt{\log 2})$. The abstract explicitly says that the paper resolves Erdős Problem 768, so this seems to answer the question on the page affirmatively and determine the leading constant.

It is important to note that the papers by Eric Li are AI-generated and I see, at least as of this moment, no reason to believe that a human has read and understood the arguments. As with all AI-generated proofs, we will have to wait for the proof (which should ideally be rewritten into something more humanly readable) to be verified by a trusted source.

A standard check with GPT-5.5 pro finds no errors in the proof logic and states it is sufficient to resolve the problem. Though, as Woett has stated, these proofs are essentially AI-generated. If I may comment on the proof itself, the exposition of the proof is okay, though can definitely be improved and in parts be simplified. Additionally, there doesn't seem to be any deeper mathematics presented here, apart from applying known techniques.
